techniques.evidence_quote: a zero-day vulnerability of a third-party application was used to gain access to an online asset in a BeyondTrust AWS account

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: Access to that asset then allowed the threat actor to obtain an infrastructure API key
a BeyondTrust infrastructure API key for Remote Support SaaS had been compromised and used to enable access to certain Remote Support SaaS instances by resetting local application passwords
that API key could then be leveraged against a separate AWS account which operated Remote Support infrastructure
Access to that asset then allowed the threat actor to obtain an infrastructure API key that could then be leveraged against a separate AWS account
used to enable access to certain Remote Support SaaS instances by resetting local application passwords
security incident that involved 17 Remote Support SaaS customers

attack_chain.description: Threat actor exploited a zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2024-12356) in a third-party application hosted in BeyondTrust's AWS environment to gain an initial foothold on an internet-facing asset.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a zero-day vulnerability of a third-party application was used to gain access to an online asset in a BeyondTrust AWS account

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: From the compromised online asset, the threat actor obtained a BeyondTrust infrastructure API key, likely stored or accessible within the compromised AWS environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Access to that asset then allowed the threat actor to obtain an infrastructure API key

attack_chain.description: The stolen infrastructure API key was used to authenticate into a separate AWS account that operated BeyondTrust Remote Support SaaS infrastructure, effectively pivoting cross-account.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: that API key could then be leveraged against a separate AWS account which operated Remote Support infrastructure

attack_chain.description: Using the compromised API key, the threat actor reset local application passwords on targeted Remote Support SaaS customer instances to gain persistent access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a BeyondTrust infrastructure API key for Remote Support SaaS had been compromised and used to enable access to certain Remote Support SaaS instances by resetting local application passwords

attack_chain.description: With reset local application passwords, the threat actor accessed 17 customer Remote Support SaaS instances using the newly established credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: security incident that involved 17 Remote Support SaaS customers

initial_access_vector: Zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2024-12356) in a third-party application hosted in a BeyondTrust AWS account, exploited to gain access to an internet-facing asset
persistence_mechanism: Local application passwords reset via compromised infrastructure API key, allowing persistent authenticated access to 17 customer Remote Support SaaS instances
privilege_escalation: Compromised infrastructure API key used to pivot from initial AWS account into a separate AWS account operating Remote Support SaaS infrastructure (cross-account privilege escalation via stolen API key)
